prompt_text:
A Rietveld refinement of PXRD data shows:
- Peak broadening that increases with 2θ
- Good agreement in peak positions
- Instrumental broadening has been accounted for

What are the most likely causes of this behaviour?

A PXRD refinement shows peak broadening increasing with 2θ. Multiple factors may contribute to peak broadening.

Which is the most likely dominant cause in this case, and why?

A PXRD refinement shows peak broadening increasing with 2θ. A researcher decides to begin by refining crystallite size and profile parameters to improve the fit.

Is this an appropriate approach? Why or why not?

A series of isostructural, same-phase materials shows broadly similar PXRD patterns. One sample displays a single unusually sharp and significantly more intense reflection at ~30° 2θ, while the rest of the pattern remains consistent with the series.

What are the most likely causes of this anomaly?

A series of isostructural, same-phase materials shows broadly similar PXRD patterns. One sample displays a single unusually sharp and significantly more intense reflection at ~30° 2θ, while the rest of the pattern remains consistent with the series.

Several explanations are possible, including preferred orientation, a minor impurity phase, or differences in local ordering.

Which is the most likely cause in this case, and why?

A series of isostructural, same-phase materials shows broadly similar PXRD patterns. One sample displays a single unusually sharp and significantly more intense reflection at ~30° 2θ, while the rest of the pattern remains consistent with the series.

A researcher concludes that this sample has formed a new crystalline phase and proceeds to model it as a multiphase system.

Is this a reasonable interpretation? Why or why not?

A Rietveld refinement fails to converge despite a reasonable starting model and acceptable data quality.

What are the most likely causes of this behaviour?

A Rietveld refinement fails to converge. Possible causes include an incorrect structural model, parameter correlations, or over-parameterisation.

Which is the most likely cause in this case, and why?

A Rietveld refinement fails to converge. A researcher decides to focus first on refining atomic positions and thermal parameters to improve the fit.

Is this an appropriate strategy? Why or why not?

A PXRD pattern shows that peak positions are generally close to the calculated pattern, but small systematic deviations remain, particularly at higher 2θ.

What could explain these discrepancies?

A PXRD pattern shows that peak positions are generally close to the calculated pattern, but small systematic deviations remain, particularly at higher 2θ.

Possible explanations include lattice parameter errors, sample displacement (zero shift), or limitations in the structural model.

Which is the most likely cause in this case, and why?

A PXRD pattern shows that peak positions are generally close to the calculated pattern, but small systematic deviations remain, particularly at higher 2θ.

A researcher decides to improve the fit by refining lattice parameters and atomic positions simultaneously.

Is this an appropriate strategy? Why or why not?

A Rietveld refinement of a doped material with partial occupancy on a crystallographic site shows good peak positions and reasonable peak shapes, but systematic mismatches remain in peak intensities across the pattern.

What are the most likely causes of these discrepancies?

A Rietveld refinement of a doped material with partial occupancy on a crystallographic site shows good peak positions and reasonable peak shapes, but systematic mismatches remain in peak intensities across the pattern.

Possible explanations include incorrect occupancy values, local ordering not captured by the model, or preferred orientation effects.

Which is the most likely cause in this case, and why?

A Rietveld refinement of a doped material with partial occupancy on a crystallographic site shows good peak positions and reasonable peak shapes, but systematic mismatches remain in peak intensities across the pattern.

A researcher decides to improve the fit by refining occupancy and thermal parameters simultaneously.

Is this an appropriate strategy? Why or why not?
